lessons.title: I, Nephi, Having Been Born

verse.hebrew: אֲנִי נְפִי נוֹלַדְתִּי לְהוֹרִים טוֹבִים עַל־כֵּן לֻמַּדְתִּי בְּכָל־תּוֹרַת אָבִי
verse.translit: ani Nefi noladti le-horim tovim al-ken lumadti be-chol-torat avi

lessons.gloss: I, Nephi, having been born to good parents, therefore was I taught in all the learning of my father.
lessons.focus: Introduce the Niphal noladti (I was born). Introduce לִמֵּד in Pual lumadti (I was taught). Reinforce תּוֹרָה (Torah, learning, instruction) as the noun of י־ר־ה. The child immediately recognizes תּוֹרָה from Journey 3 lesson 40. This recognition is the first taste of convergent validation.

games.raw: Chant Along, Convergent Validation Highlight (the word torah glows to mark recognition across Standard Works), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recognizes torah from Journey 3 and recites the opening verse.

lessons.title: Many Afflictions in My Days
lessons.gloss: And I, having seen many afflictions in my days.
lessons.focus: Introduce צָרָה (affliction, distress) in feminine plural. Reinforce ר־א־ה in 1cs perfect with emphatic pronoun.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 1b and identifies the emphatic va-ani construction.

lessons.title: The Goodness of God
lessons.gloss: And I have learned also the goodness of the LORD in all my days.
lessons.focus: Introduce ל־מ־ד in Qal 1cs perfect. Reinforce the construct tov YHVH (goodness of the LORD).

games.raw: Chant Along, Binyan Builder (Qal vs Pual of l-m-d from lesson 1), Construct Chain Builder, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child distinguishes lamad (he learned) from lumad (he was taught).

lessons.title: A Record of My Proceedings
lessons.gloss: Therefore I make a record of my proceedings in my days.
lessons.focus: Introduce מַעֲשֶׂה (deed, act) in plural with 1cs suffix. Introduce סֵפֶר (book, scroll) — the child connects this to the root ס־פ־ר learned in Journey 2 lesson 11. Another moment of recognition.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Noun Family Highlight (sofer scribe, sefer book, lispor to count), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child names three nouns / verbs from the s-p-r family.

lessons.title: Lehi Prayed to the LORD

verse.hebrew: עַל־כֵּן לֵחִי אָבִי הָלַךְ וְהִתְפַּלֵּל אֶל־יְהוָה בְּכָל־לֵב בְּעַד עַמּוֹ
verse.translit: al-ken Lechi avi halach ve-hitpalel el-YHVH be-chol-lev be'ad amo

lessons.gloss: Therefore Lehi my father went and prayed to the LORD with all his heart on behalf of his people.
lessons.focus: Introduce the Hithpael hitpalel (he prayed). Introduce פ־ל־ל (to pray, intercede, judge). Introduce the preposition בְּעַד (on behalf of).

games.raw: Chant Along, Binyan Builder (Hithpael prominent), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child forms Hithpael of p-l-l and recognizes the prayer root.

lessons.title: A Pillar of Fire Came
lessons.gloss: And there came a pillar of fire and stood before him upon a rock.
lessons.focus: Introduce עַמּוּד (pillar). Introduce צוּר (rock, stronghold). The child recognizes amud-esh from Exodus (pillar of fire in the wilderness). Strong intertextual echo.

games.raw: Chant Along, Intertextual Echo Highlight (pillar of fire echoes Exodus), Construct Chain Builder, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child names the Exodus parallel (pillar of fire leading Israel in the wilderness).

lessons.title: Lehi Saw and Heard Much
lessons.gloss: And he saw and heard much.
lessons.focus: Short consolidation verse. Reinforce ר־א־ה and ש־מ־ע, the two verbs of prophetic reception.

games.raw: Chant Along, Memorization Reward, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recites the verse from memory.

lessons.title: He Cast Himself on His Bed

verse.hebrew: וַיִּפֹּל עַל־מִטָּתוֹ כְּאִישׁ נִרְדָּם מֵרֹב עֲצַב
verse.translit: va-yipol al-mitato ke-ish nirdam me-rov atzav

lessons.gloss: And he fell upon his bed as a man overcome from great sorrow.
lessons.focus: Reinforce נ־פ־ל from Journey 2 lesson 57. Introduce the Niphal nirdam (fast asleep, overcome, from ר־ד־מ). Introduce the adjective רָב (great, much).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 7 aloud with minimal support.

lessons.title: Take Thy Family and Depart

verse.hebrew: וַיְצַו אֹתוֹ יְהוָה לַאמֹר קַח־נָא אֶת־מִשְׁפַּחְתְּךָ וְצֵא לַמִּדְבָּר
verse.translit: va-yetzav oto YHVH lemor kach-na et-mishpachtecha ve-tze la-midbar

lessons.gloss: And the LORD commanded him, saying, Take now your family and go forth into the wilderness.
lessons.focus: Introduce the Piel va-yetzav (he commanded, from ts-v-h, reinforced from Journey 3 lesson 64). Reinforce the imperative kach from Journey 2 lesson 20. Introduce מִדְבָּר (wilderness). Strong echo of Genesis 12:1 (lech-lecha).

games.raw: Chant Along, Intertextual Echo Highlight (Abrahamic call parallel), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child identifies the Genesis 12:1 parallel without being told.

lessons.title: He Was Obedient to the Word

verse.hebrew: וַיְהִי שֹׁמֵעַ בְּקוֹל יְהוָה עַל־כֵּן לֹא עָשָׂה דָבָר מִבַּד אֲשֶׁר צִוָּהוּ יְהוָה
verse.translit: va-yehi shomea be-kol YHVH al-ken lo asa davar mi-bad asher tzivahu YHVH

lessons.gloss: And he was hearkening to the voice of the LORD, therefore he did not a thing apart from what the LORD commanded him.
lessons.focus: Reinforce the idiom shomea be-kol (hearkening to the voice, i.e. obeying). Reinforce the Piel perfect with 3ms suffix tzivahu.

games.raw: Chant Along, Idiom Highlight (obedience idiom from Exodus), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recognizes the obedience idiom from Journey 3.

lessons.title: He Left His House
lessons.gloss: And he left his house and his inheritance and his gold and his silver.
lessons.focus: Introduce ע־ז־ב (to leave, forsake). Introduce נַחֲלָה (inheritance), a weighty covenant term. Reinforce זָהָב (gold, from Journey 1) and כֶּסֶף (silver, from Journey 2).

games.raw: Chant Along, Possessive Suffix Chain Builder, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child forms a chain of possessive suffixes and recognizes nachala as a covenant term.

lessons.title: They Pitched Tents in the Valley
lessons.gloss: And he pitched his tent in the valley of the river.
lessons.focus: Reinforce נ־ט־ה (to pitch, stretch). Introduce גַּיְא (valley) and נָהָר (river).

games.raw: Chant Along, Construct Chain Builder, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 6 with minimal support.

lessons.title: Nephi Had Great Desires

verse.hebrew: אֲנִי נְפִי הָיוּ־לִי תְּשׁוּקוֹת רַבּוֹת לָדַעַת אֶת־סִתְרֵי יְהוָה
verse.translit: ani Nefi hayu-li teshukot rabot ladaat et-sitrei YHVH

lessons.gloss: I, Nephi, had great desires to know the mysteries of the LORD.
lessons.focus: Introduce תְּשׁוּקָה (desire, from sh-v-k). Introduce סוֹד / סֵתֶר (mystery, secret counsel). Reinforce the infinitive construct la-da'at (to know).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Infinitive Builder, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 16.

lessons.title: I Cried to the LORD
lessons.gloss: And I cried unto the LORD.
lessons.focus: Introduce ז־ע־ק (to cry out). Short, memorable verse.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 16b from memory.

lessons.title: The LORD Visited Me
lessons.gloss: And the LORD visited me and softened my heart.
lessons.focus: Reinforce פ־ק־ד from Journey 2 lesson 58 (a covenant-memory verb). Introduce ר־כ־כ (to be soft) in Hiphil (soften).

games.raw: Chant Along, Covenant Vocabulary Review, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recognizes p-k-d as the covenant-visiting verb from Joseph's deathbed.

lessons.title: Blessed Art Thou, Nephi
lessons.gloss: Blessed are you, Nephi, because of your faith.
lessons.focus: Introduce the blessing formula baruch ata. Introduce אֱמוּנָה (faith, faithfulness) from א־מ־נ, the noun form of the root learned in Journey 2 lesson 10 and reinforced as emet in Journey 3 lesson 54. Convergent validation: faith and truth share a root.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (emunah, emet, ne'eman all from a-m-n), Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child names three words from the a-m-n family and connects them.

lessons.title: Laman and Lemuel Murmured
lessons.gloss: And Laman and Lemuel murmured against their father.
lessons.focus: Introduce ל־ו־נ (to murmur, complain). Reinforce 3mp imperfect with vav-consecutive.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (murmuring will reappear in Numbers), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 5 and identifies the 3mp vav-consecutive form.

lessons.title: I Will Go and Do

verse.hebrew: אֵלֵךְ וְאֶעֱשֶׂה אֶת־הַדָּבָר אֲשֶׁר צִוָּה יְהוָה
verse.translit: elech ve-e'eseh et-ha-davar asher tziva YHVH

lessons.gloss: I will go and do what the LORD has commanded.
lessons.focus: The most famous verse in 1 Nephi. Reinforce 1cs imperfect of h-l-k and a-s-h. Reinforce Piel perfect of ts-v-h. This verse is a Journey-defining memorization target.

games.raw: Chant Along, Memorization Reward (highest priority), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 7 from memory in Hebrew. This is a major milestone.

lessons.title: For the LORD Giveth No Command

verse.hebrew: כִּי יְהוָה לֹא־יִתֵּן מִצְוָה לִבְנֵי־אָדָם כִּי אִם־יְכִין לָהֶם דֶּרֶךְ לַעֲשׂוֹת אֲשֶׁר צִוָּה
verse.translit: ki YHVH lo-yiten mitzva li-vnei-adam ki im-yachin lahem derech la'asot asher tziva

lessons.gloss: For the LORD gives no commandment to the children of men unless he prepares a way for them to do what he has commanded.
lessons.focus: Reinforce מִצְוָה from Journey 3 lesson 64. Introduce the Hiphil יָכִין (he prepares, establishes, from כ־ו־נ).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Convergent Validation Highlight (mitzvah glows), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 7b and recognizes mitzvah from Journey 3.

lessons.title: We Went Up to Jerusalem
lessons.gloss: And we went up to Jerusalem.
lessons.focus: Reinforce ע־ל־ה in 1cp with vav-consecutive. Reinforce the directional he on Jerusalem.

games.raw: Chant Along, Directional He Match, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child forms 1cp imperfect with vav-consecutive.

lessons.title: Laban Sought Our Lives
lessons.gloss: And Laban seized all our property and sought our lives.
lessons.focus: Introduce ג־ז־ל (to seize, rob). Introduce ב־ק־שׁ in Piel (to seek). Reinforce נֶפֶשׁ in plural with 1cp suffix.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Binyan Builder (ב־ק־שׁ Piel), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 25 and recognizes the idiom "to seek the soul / life" as hostile intent.

lessons.title: The Spirit Constrained Me
lessons.gloss: And the Spirit of the LORD constrained me to slay Laban.
lessons.focus: Introduce רוּחַ (spirit, wind, breath). Introduce the Hiphil hetzikani (constrained me, from ts-v-k). Introduce ה־ר־ג (to slay).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 10 and names ruach as a key term (breath / wind / spirit).

lessons.title: Better That One Man Should Perish

verse.hebrew: טוֹב כִּי יֹאבַד אִישׁ אֶחָד מִלְּאַבֵּד אֻמָּה שְׁלֵמָה בְּלֹא אֱמוּנָה וּמָרָה
verse.translit: tov ki yovad ish echad mi-le-abed uma shelema be-lo emuna u-mara

lessons.gloss: Better that one man should perish than that a whole nation should dwindle in unbelief and perish.
lessons.focus: Introduce א־ב־ד (to perish, be lost). Introduce אֻמָּה (nation, people) and שָׁלֵם (whole, complete).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 13 and identifies the root sh-l-m family (shalem, shalom, shalem).

lessons.title: I Smote Off His Head
lessons.gloss: And I cut off his head with his sword.
lessons.focus: Reinforce כ־ר־ת (to cut) from Journey 2 lesson 12. Introduce חֶרֶב (sword). Reinforce רֹאשׁ (head).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (note the intensity-range of karat from covenant-cutting to beheading), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 18 and connects the root k-r-t to both covenant-cutting and violent cutting.

lessons.title: I Saw in My Dream
lessons.gloss: Behold, I dreamed a dream.
lessons.focus: Reinforce ח־ל־מ (to dream) from Journey 2 lesson 33. The cognate accusative construction (dreamed a dream) is a classic Hebrew figura etymologica.

games.raw: Chant Along, Cognate Accusative Highlight, Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 2 and identifies the cognate accusative.

lessons.title: I Beheld a Man

verse.hebrew: וָאֵרֶא אִישׁ וְהוּא לָבוּשׁ בִּגְלִים לְבָנִים
verse.translit: va-ere ish ve-hu lavush bgadim levanim

lessons.gloss: And I beheld a man, and he was clothed in white robes.
lessons.focus: Introduce ל־ב־שׁ (to clothe, wear). Introduce בֶּגֶד (garment) and לָבָן (white, cognate with the Laban name learned earlier).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 5.

lessons.title: He Spake to Me and Bade Me Follow
lessons.gloss: And he spoke to me and commanded me to go after him.
lessons.focus: Reinforce ד־ב־ר in Piel. Reinforce צ־ו־ה in Piel with 1cs suffix. Introduce אַחַר (after).

games.raw: Chant Along, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 6 and identifies two Piel verbs.

lessons.title: A Dark and Dreary Wilderness
lessons.gloss: And I walked in a dark and desolate wilderness.
lessons.focus: Introduce the Hithpael ethalech (I walked about, iterative). Introduce חָשׁוּךְ (dark, from ch-sh-k) and שָׁמֵם (desolate, from sh-m-m).

roots.raw: ח־שׁ־כ (to be dark), שׁ־מ־מ (to be desolate).

games.raw: Chant Along, Binyan Builder, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 7.

lessons.title: A Tree Whose Fruit Was Desirable

verse.hebrew: וָאֵרֶא עֵץ אֲשֶׁר פִּרְיוֹ נֶחְמָד לְהָשִׂים בָּשָׂר שָׂמֵחַ
verse.translit: va-ere etz asher piryo nechmad lehasim basar sameach

lessons.gloss: And I beheld a tree whose fruit was desirable to make one happy.
lessons.focus: Strong intertextual echo of Genesis 2:9 and 3:6 (the Tree of Life and the forbidden tree). Introduce נֶחְמָד (desirable, Niphal participle of ch-m-d). Introduce שָׂמֵחַ (happy, from s-m-ch).

games.raw: Chant Along, Genesis Echo Highlight (the tree whose fruit is desirable echoes Genesis 3:6), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child names the Genesis parallel.

lessons.title: I Partook of the Fruit
lessons.gloss: And I ate of its fruit, and it was exceedingly sweet.
lessons.focus: Reinforce the eating-of-fruit construction (echoing Eden). Introduce מָתוֹק (sweet, reinforcing m-t-k from Journey 3 lesson 40).

games.raw: Chant Along, Genesis Echo Highlight, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 11 and names the Eden parallel (eating fruit from a tree).

lessons.title: I Saw a River of Water

verse.hebrew: וָאֵרֶא נְהַר־מַיִם וְהוּא רָץ לְצַד הָעֵץ
verse.translit: va-ere nehar-mayim ve-hu ratz le-tzad ha-etz

lessons.gloss: And I saw a river of water, and it ran alongside the tree.
lessons.focus: Introduce ר־ו־צ (to run). Reinforce נָהָר in construct.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Construct Chain Builder, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 13.

lessons.title: A Rod of Iron

verse.hebrew: וָאֵרֶא שֵׁבֶט בַּרְזֶל וְהוּא הוֹלֵךְ לְאֹרֶךְ הַנָּהָר
verse.translit: va-ere shevet barzel ve-hu holech le-orech ha-nahar

lessons.gloss: And I beheld a rod of iron, and it extended along the river.
lessons.focus: Introduce בַּרְזֶל (iron). Reinforce שֵׁבֶט from Journey 2 lesson 55 (scepter of Judah); here it is a rod. Introduce אֹרֶךְ (length).

games.raw: Chant Along, Construct Chain Builder, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 19.

lessons.title: A Great and Spacious Building

verse.hebrew: וָאֵרֶא בַּיִת גָּדוֹל וְרָחָב עֹמֵד כְּאִלּוּ בָּאַוֵּר מֵעַל לָאָרֶץ
verse.translit: va-ere bayit gadol ve-rachav omed ke-ilu ba-avir me-al la-aretz

lessons.gloss: And I beheld a great and spacious building standing as if in the air above the earth.
lessons.focus: Introduce רָחָב (wide, spacious). Introduce אַוֵּר (air, atmosphere). Introduce the hypothetical particle כְּאִלּוּ (as if).

games.raw: Chant Along, Hypothetical Builder (as if X), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 26.

lessons.title: Hold Fast to the Rod
lessons.gloss: Hold fast to the rod of iron and go unto the tree.
lessons.focus: Introduce א־ח־ז (to hold fast, grasp) as Qal imperative. Reinforce lech. A weighty exhortation.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Imperative Chain Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 30 from memory and names its covenant imagery.

lessons.title: My Soul Delighteth
lessons.gloss: Behold, my soul delights in the word of the LORD.
lessons.focus: Introduce ע־נ־ג (to delight) in Hithpael. Reinforce the Hithpael prefix pattern. Note: Hithpael titaneg carries a reflexive-intensive sense (delights itself).

games.raw: Chant Along, Binyan Builder (Hithpael), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child forms Hithpael of a familiar root.

lessons.title: Nevertheless I Know in Whom I Have Trusted

verse.hebrew: אַף עַל־פִּי־כֵן אֲנִי יוֹדֵעַ בְּמִי בָטַחְתִּי
verse.translit: af al-pi-chen ani yodea be-mi batachti

lessons.gloss: Nevertheless, I know in whom I have trusted.
lessons.focus: Introduce ב־ט־ח (to trust). Introduce the concessive idiom af al-pi-chen (nevertheless). Reinforce the interrogative mi as a relative (whom).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (Hebrew has multiple trust-roots: a-m-n, b-t-ch), Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child distinguishes b-t-ch (to trust) from a-m-n (to be firm / believe).

lessons.title: My God Hath Been My Support

verse.hebrew: אֱלֹהַי הָיָה לִי לְמִסְעָד בִּימֵי נְדוֹדִי בַּמִּדְבָּר
verse.translit: Elohai haya li le-mis'ad be-yemei nedudi ba-midbar

lessons.gloss: My God has been my support in the days of my wandering in the wilderness.
lessons.focus: Introduce מִסְעָד (support, from s-ayin-d, to sustain). Introduce נ־ד־ד (to wander) in verbal noun form nedudim.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 20.

lessons.title: Preserved Me Upon the Waters
lessons.gloss: He kept me upon the face of the great sea.
lessons.focus: Reinforce ש־מ־ר (to keep, guard). Reinforce yam as the sea, now a large symbolic sea.

games.raw: Chant Along, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 20b fluently.

lessons.title: Awake, My Soul
lessons.gloss: Awake, my soul, be grieved no longer.
lessons.focus: Introduce the Hiphil imperative hakitza (awake!) from k-v-tz. Reinforce al-te'atzvi (negative Niphal imperative feminine singular, from Journey 3 lesson 52 in 2mp form). Poetic apostrophe to the soul.

games.raw: Chant Along, Binyan Builder, Gender Sort, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 28 and identifies the feminine singular negative imperative.

lessons.title: Rejoice, O My Heart
lessons.gloss: Rejoice, my heart, and fall no more.
lessons.focus: Introduce ג־י־ל (to rejoice, exult). Reinforce n-p-l in negative feminine imperative.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (joy-verbs: s-m-ch, g-y-l, r-n-n), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child names three joy-verbs.

lessons.title: I Will Trust in Thee Forever
lessons.gloss: I will trust in you forever.
lessons.focus: Short, weighty covenant-trust verse. Reinforce b-t-ch in 1cs imperfect.

games.raw: Chant Along, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 34 from memory.

lessons.title: O Lord, Wilt Thou Redeem My Soul
lessons.gloss: O LORD, wilt thou redeem my soul?
lessons.focus: Introduce ג־א־ל (to redeem, act as kinsman-redeemer). This is the foundational go'el root, central to Chris's Covenant King commentary on Mosiah. The child meets it here for the first time in restoration scripture; it will recur heavily in Journey 6.

games.raw: Chant Along, Covenant Vocabulary Highlight (goel / geulah glow with special treatment), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recognizes goel as the kinsman-redeemer root and begins to track it.

lessons.title: Hearken, O House of Jacob

verse.hebrew: שִׁמְעוּ זֹאת בֵּית־יַעֲקֹב הַנִּקְרָאִים בְּשֵׁם יִשְׂרָאֵל
verse.translit: shim'u zot beit-Ya'akov ha-nikra'im be-shem Yisrael

lessons.gloss: Hear this, house of Jacob, who are called by the name of Israel.
lessons.focus: Reinforce the 2mp imperative shim'u. Introduce the Niphal participle plural ha-nikra'im (those who are called).

games.raw: Chant Along, Binyan Builder, Participle Match, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads Isaiah 48:1 in its Sefer Nephi form.

lessons.title: I Am He, I Am the First

verse.hebrew: אֲנִי־הוּא אֲנִי רִאשׁוֹן אַף אֲנִי אַחֲרוֹן
verse.translit: ani-hu ani rishon af ani acharon

lessons.gloss: I am he, I am the first, also I am the last.
lessons.focus: The divine self-identification. Reinforce rishon and introduce אַחֲרוֹן (last). Echo of Isaiah 44:6 and Revelation 1:8 (for parents, this is the Alpha-Omega identity in Hebrew).

games.raw: Chant Along, Memorization Reward, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recites the divine self-identification.

lessons.title: Hearken, O Ye House of Israel

verse.hebrew: הַקְשִׁיבוּ אִיִּים אֵלַי וְהַאֲזִינוּ לְאֻמִּים מֵרָחוֹק
verse.translit: hakshivu iyim elai ve-ha'azinu le'umim me-rachok

lessons.gloss: Listen, O isles, unto me, and hearken, ye peoples, from afar.
lessons.focus: Introduce ק־שׁ־ב (to pay attention) and א־ז־נ (to give ear) in Hiphil. Two parallel hearing-verbs in poetic synonymy.

roots.raw: ק־שׁ־ב, א־ז־נ (both Hiphil: pay attention / give ear).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (hearing-verbs: sh-m-a, k-sh-v, a-z-n), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child names three hearing-verbs.

lessons.title: Can a Woman Forget Her Nursing Child
lessons.gloss: Can a woman forget her nursing child, from compassion on the son of her womb?
lessons.focus: Introduce ש־כ־ח (to forget). Reinforce ר־ח־מ from Journey 3 lesson 53 (thirteen attributes). Introduce בֶּטֶן (womb).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child connects rachem (compassion) back to rachum (merciful) from Journey 3.

lessons.title: I Have Graven Thee Upon the Palms
lessons.gloss: Behold, I have graven you upon the palms.
lessons.focus: Introduce ח־ק־ק (reinforced from Journey 2 lesson 55, now as graven). Introduce כַּף (palm, hollow of hand) in dual. A beloved verse.

games.raw: Chant Along, Dual Match, Memorization Reward, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 16 from memory.

lessons.title: Arise, Shine
lessons.gloss: Arise, shine, for your light has come.
lessons.focus: Feminine imperatives kumi and ori. Reinforce the name of Aleph Trail (ori = my light / shine!). A memorable liturgical verse.

games.raw: Chant Along, Feminine Imperative Match, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites the arise-shine verse.

lessons.title: All Nations Shall Flow Unto It
lessons.gloss: And all the nations shall flow unto it.
lessons.focus: Introduce נ־ה־ר (to flow, from which nahar, river, also derives).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (nahar as river, naharu as they flowed), Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse and names the noun-verb connection for n-h-r.

lessons.title: They Shall Beat Their Swords
lessons.gloss: And they shall beat their swords into plowshares.
lessons.focus: Introduce כ־ת־ת (to beat, crush) in Piel. Reinforce chevev (sword). Introduce אֵת (plowshare, a farming implement).

games.raw: Chant Along, Memorization Reward, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child recites the swords-to-plowshares verse.

lessons.title: Build a Ship
lessons.gloss: Build for yourself a ship after the pattern which I will show you.
lessons.focus: Reinforce ב־נ־ה. Introduce אֳנִיָּה (ship) and תַּכְנִית (pattern, model).

games.raw: Chant Along, Verse Builder, Intertextual Echo Highlight ("show you a pattern" echoes the tabernacle instructions in Exodus 25).

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 8 and names the tabernacle-pattern parallel.

lessons.title: The Lord Remembers His People
lessons.gloss: And the LORD remembers his people in the day of their affliction.
lessons.focus: Strong covenant-memory language. Reinforce z-k-r from Journey 3 lesson 7 and lesson 28. Reinforce tzara from Journey 4 lesson 2.

games.raw: Chant Along, Covenant Vocabulary Review, Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites verse 30 and connects it to the Exodus covenant-remembering.

lessons.title: Moses Smote the Rock

verse.hebrew: וַיַּךְ מֹשֶׁה אֶת־הַצּוּר בְּמַטֵּהוּ וַיֵּצְאוּ מַיִם
verse.translit: va-yach Moshe et-ha-tzur be-matehu va-yetze'u mayim

lessons.gloss: And Moses smote the rock with his rod, and waters came forth.
lessons.focus: Introduce נ־כ־ה (to strike, smite) in Hiphil vav-consecutive (va-yach). A classic Exodus image embedded in Nephi.

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Exodus Echo Highlight, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child names the Exodus parallel (Moses striking the rock in Exodus 17).

lessons.title: They Tempted God in the Wilderness
lessons.gloss: And they tempted God in the wilderness.
lessons.focus: Introduce נ־ס־ה (to test, try). Reinforce midbar. Echo of Numbers 14.

games.raw: Chant Along, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 30b.

lessons.title: The Lord Can Do All Things
lessons.gloss: If the LORD should speak unto the earth to be, it would be.
lessons.focus: A concise statement of divine creative power. Echo of Genesis 1.

games.raw: Chant Along, Genesis 1 Echo Highlight, Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child recites the verse and connects it to Genesis 1 "let there be."

lessons.title: The Winds Ceased
lessons.gloss: And the winds ceased and the sea became calm.
lessons.focus: Introduce פ־ס־ק (to cease) and שׁ־ק־ט (to be quiet).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort, Verse Builder.

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 21.

lessons.title: We Arrived at the Land of Promise
lessons.gloss: And we arrived at the land of promise.
lessons.focus: Reinforce נ־ג־ע (reinforced from Journey 2 lesson 33). Introduce הַבְטָחָה (promise, from b-t-ch, reinforced from Journey 4 lesson 36 as trust).

games.raw: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort (trust and promise share b-t-ch), Verse Builder, Memorization Reward.

lessons.exit: Child connects the root b-t-ch across trust and promise.

lessons.title: We Pitched Tents
lessons.gloss: And we pitched our tents.
lessons.focus: Reinforce n-t-a (pitching tents, echoing Abraham and Lehi's earlier pitching). Reinforce ohel in plural with 1cp suffix.

games.raw: Chant Along, Verse Builder, Intertextual Echo Highlight (tent-pitching as patriarchal signature).

lessons.exit: Child reads verse 23b and names the patriarchal parallel.

lessons.title: Consolidation Before Exit
lessons.gloss: Consolidation lesson before Journey 4 exit. The child reads 1 Nephi 3:7, 1 Nephi 8:30, and 2 Nephi 4:34 (three memorization targets) from memory.
lessons.focus: No new content.

games.raw: Memorization Showcase, Chant Along.

lessons.exit: Child recites all three verses from memory smoothly.

lessons.title: Journey 4 Exit: 1 Nephi 8:1-33

verse.ref: Lehi's full Tree of Life vision read aloud

lessons.gloss: Journey 4 exit. The child reads 1 Nephi 8:1-33 aloud in full, the complete Tree of Life vision narrative.
lessons.focus: All Journey 1-4 grammar and vocabulary consolidated. No new content.

games.raw: Final Scroll Reader, Memorization Showcase, Parent Submission Flow.

lessons.exit: Child records themselves reading 1 Nephi 8:1-33 aloud. Parent approves. Journey 4 complete. Journey 5 (Land and Kings) unlocks.
